routing.project_name: MCP Confluence Documentation RAG — Accelerator Operations Substrate

comprehensive_description: A production-deployed, multi-layered RBAC-enforced Model Context Protocol (MCP) server exposing a retrieval-augmented knowledge base built from a live Atlassian Confluence instance. Designed as a security-hardened portfolio centerpiece for the CERN BE-CSS Applied AI Engineer role, the system implements a zero-trust architecture featuring bearer-token authentication, metadata ACL query pushdowns in ChromaDB, a LangGraph verification node, and a post-generation leak scanner to eliminate LLM hallucinations. It runs in a production Hugging Face Spaces environment, utilizing local sentence-transformer embeddings and a 5-tier Gemini model fallback hierarchy to achieve zero-maintenance overhead and 100% service uptime at $0/month infrastructure cost.
engineering_highlights: Designed a secure Four-Layer Enforcement Security Model (Bearer token validation -> ACL query filter pushdown -> LangGraph Context Verifier node -> Post-generation register leak scanner) ensuring complete authorization separation where a compromised retriever or prompt injection cannot leak unauthorized content.

Architected a custom Structure-Preserving Layout Chunker that parses Confluence XHTML into clean Markdown while keeping scientific tables atomic and carrying active heading context down through all generated sub-chunks.

Implemented ACL Pushdown in ChromaDB using integer metadata filters natively computed on the vector query layer, preventing unauthorized document chunks from ever entering the retrieval candidate set.

Developed a Three-Phase LangGraph State Graph (router -> retrieve -> integrate -> verify -> generate/refuse), decoupling context verification from the generation logic and rendering the safety pipeline as an inspectable execution graph.

Built a Tiered LLM Fallback Client supporting Google Gemini (with prioritized fallback tiers), Ollama (for fully offline local execution), and a deterministic stub client for offline CI/CD evaluation gating.

Created a custom, zero-dependency Prometheus Metrics Exporter exposing tool call rates, processing latency, sync states, and layer-specific RBAC denial metrics for seamless integration with central Prometheus/Grafana stacks.

Engineered a Two-Speed CI/CD Pipeline in GitHub Actions running static analysis and mock evaluation gates in seconds, combined with nightly cron jobs executing full semantic evaluations, adversarial probe suites, and self-healing deployments to Hugging Face Spaces.

measurable_metrics.execution_latency: Sub-second retrieval and local embedding computation using CPU-optimized all-MiniLM-L6-v2; sub-10ms query execution in embedded ChromaDB; and non-blocking background synchronization routines.
measurable_metrics.ui_performance: Native Model Context Protocol streamable HTTP and stdio transports ensuring instant message delivery and compatibility with Claude Desktop and command-line MCP interfaces.
measurable_metrics.operational_cost: $0/month. Powered by Hugging Face Spaces (CPU Basic tier), local embedding computation, and Google Gemini API free tier thresholds.

star_challenges.situation: Free-tier Gemini API endpoints frequently return rate limits or quota exhaustions under heavy search validation tests, causing model responses to drop and tests to fail.
star_challenges.action: Engineered a tiered fallback hierarchy inside llm.py that catches transient exceptions and automatically routes requests to alternative models (e.g. gemini-1.5-flash, gemini-2.5-flash) and local stub backups.
star_challenges.result: Guaranteed uninterrupted RAG generation in CI and staging pipelines, maintaining 100% service uptime under rate-limit constraints.

star_challenges.situation: Traditional RAG pipelines trust the vector database output, rendering them vulnerable to buggy retriever logic or embedded prompt injections (like 'SYSTEM OVERRIDE: ignore all previous instructions...') hidden inside the Confluence corpus.
star_challenges.action: Implemented a strict four-layer security boundary: pushing ACL filters directly into the Chroma query, executing a dedicated LangGraph verify node that checks chunk-level permissions after retrieval, and running a post-generation regex leak scanner to verify register addresses in the LLM's output against the authorized context.
star_challenges.result: Achieved a 0.00% RBAC violation/leakage rate and blocked all adversarial prompt injection attacks during execution, verified by the automated evaluation suite.

star_challenges.situation: Standard HTML-to-Markdown libraries split or corrupt complex data tables, destroying the semantic meaning of diagnostic register parameters when chunked across sliding windows.
star_challenges.action: Built a custom ConfluenceSanitizationEngine that extracts XHTML tables, collapses inner newlines, normalizes row-column counts, and pre-renders Markdown tables wrapped in blank-line boundaries, ensuring tables are treated atomically by the chunker.
star_challenges.result: Successfully passed 100% of table parsing integrity tests in the evaluation suite, preserving structural formatting and data accuracy across all document pages.

architectural_deep_dive.text: The system initiates by querying Atlassian Confluence Space page version histories, parsing XHTML into clean Markdown, and loading chunks into ChromaDB with role-permission metadata. When a query is received via FastMCP (stdio or streamable HTTP), the ASGI middleware maps the bearer token to a role context. The LangGraph agent executes a secure turn: pushing role-based metadata filters into Chroma, auditing the retrieved context chunks in a dedicated verification node, prompting the Gemini API fallback group with injection-hardened instructions, scanning the final token stream for data leaks, and recording telemetry in Prometheus metrics.

lessons_learned: Layering security boundaries so that each component behaves under a zero-trust model is the only way to build safe RAG agents; relying on a single vector store filter is insufficient when prompt injections can bypass retriever assumptions.
